data.description:
## Context / Problem

A project's own code-review criteria (declared in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`) are supposed
to flow through the same four-pass code-review pipeline as the 13 built-in overlays, exactly as
plan-review already lets projects add criteria (registry story 5065; contract in
`docs/llm-framework.md`). They do not: the overlay merge succeeds, but the criterion is then
silently dropped at five runtime seams, so a criterion author sees a valid config and **no
execution, no routing, and no eval path** — a silent failure indistinguishable from a
mis-config. This epic supersedes bug 8a4b (the defect report) and story 2c14 (the umbrella
"complete the generic code-review project-criterion runtime seam"), taking on their scope as a
sequenced, implementable decomposition.

Root cause (already proven on 8a4b, not re-derived here): **one missing abstraction** — a
gate-keyed logical→physical criterion-id resolver plus threading the repository-effective
criteria/routing through the code-review runtime, mirroring how plan-review already does it. The
five drop points, each confirmed by reading `origin/main`:

1. `criterion_prompt_id` hardcodes the plan-review prompt prefix
   (`src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:31,42`), so a project code-review criterion resolves into the
   **plan-review** prompt namespace.
2. `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run` iterates only `req.criteria` from the static YAML and stamps
   `reviewer_id = prompt_id` with no logical criterion id
   (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:49,79`); the gate YAML injects only the 13
   built-ins (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml:77-94,109-126`), so a project
   criterion never enters Round A/B. Plan-review solves this with a dynamic fan-in
   (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:206-225`).
3. `code_review_decide` calls `registry.threshold_for` and `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria()`
   with no routing map (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:419,427`), so Pass 3 uses the
   **packaged** map and ignores project threshold/blocking/nit-suppression — even though those
   functions already accept an optional map (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py:309-343`) and
   `registry.effective_routing(repo_root)` already exists (`registry.py:171-184`).
4. `_code_review_prompt_id` resolves against a static built-in set with no `repo_root`/gate
   (`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:187-201`) and `calibrate_criterion` has only a
   plan-review branch (`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:416-420`), so project code-review ids have no
   eval path.
5. The `criteria eval` CLI admits only `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)`
   (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:143-155`), rejecting code-review criteria as unknown.

## Success Criteria

- A project code-review criterion activated in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` executes **once**
  through Round A/B, carries its **logical** `project.<name>` id on every finding it emits, and
  its project-tuned threshold/blocking/nit-suppression govern the deterministic Pass 3.
- The same logical id resolves through the eval/calibration path and is admitted by the
  `criteria eval` CLI; an id activated in exactly one gate resolves to that gate, and an id
  active in both gates is rejected as ambiguous.
- An overlay-absent repo behaves **byte-identically** to today (delegation, not rip-and-replace),
  and the 13 built-in overlays are unchanged (parity).
- The stable contract is documented in `docs/llm-framework.md`.
- Scope is the **LLM-tier** four-pass finder path (`round_a`/`round_b`); DET/detector-tier
  criteria are out of scope.

## Decomposition

Six children, ordered by real dependencies, landed as a Gerrit stack in this order. Each child is
single-concern and edits a disjoint file set (the shared test file
`tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` grows one slice per child, RED→GREEN within that
child's own change so every stacked change lands green). The gate-keyed resolver is first because
the other seams consume it; children that rely on it cite `[rebar:<resolver-id>]` over a direct
`depends_on` edge.

1. **Gate-keyed criterion-id resolver** — make `criterion_prompt_id` gate-aware in
   `criteria/ids.py`; foundation for all others. (no dependencies)
2. **Round-A fan-in + logical-id attribution** — inject project criteria into the code-review
   batch and stamp the logical id; depends on (1).
3. **Pass-3 effective routing** — thread `registry.effective_routing(repo_root)` into the Pass-3
   resolvers; depends on (2) (needs a stamped project finding to route).
4. **Eval-path admission** — gate-aware resolution in `eval_solver.py`/`eval.py`; depends on (1).
5. **CLI admission** — admit one-gate code-review ids, reject ambiguous, in
   `_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`; depends on (4).
6. **Docs contract** — document the code-review project-criterion contract in
   `docs/llm-framework.md`; depends on (1)–(5).

No two children touch the same production file; child (3) is the only consumer of the
already-existing `effective_routing` resolver, so no child re-implements it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All six child tickets exist under this epic, each `depends_on`-linked in the order above,
      and each passes `rebar review-plan <child>` with no blocking finding; verified by
      `rebar list --parent <epic>` showing six children and a PASS attestation on each.
- [ ] Every root-cause seam (1)–(5) above is covered by exactly one child and the seams do not
      overlap; verified by cross-checking each child's `## Scope` against the five drop points.
- [ ] The epic supersedes story 2c14 and bug 8a4b via `supersedes` links, and both are closed;
      verified by `rebar show 2c14` / `rebar show 8a4b` reporting `status=closed` and the epic's
      links listing both `supersedes` edges.
data.title: Thread project code-review criteria through the four-pass code-review seam
